passage:
As used in the text, what does the word "gather" most nearly mean?
The following text is adapted from Jhumpa Lahiri's 2003 novel *The Namesake*. Ashima and her husband, Ashoke, are traveling from the United States to India.
Ashoke puts his hand over hers as the plane begins to <u>gather</u> speed. And then Boston tilts away and they ascend effortlessly over a blackened Atlantic.
©2003 by Jhumpa Lahiri

choices.label: A
choices.text: Gain

choices.label: B
choices.text: Doubt

choices.label: C
choices.text: Welcome

choices.label: D
choices.text: Force

test_name: October 2025 US Form A SAT English Module 1
passage:
Which choice best describes the function of the underlined sentence in the text as a whole?
In 2013 Bridie J. M. Allan and colleagues published a study concluding that ocean acidification has a strong effect on the behavior of *Pomacentrus amboinensis*, a species of fish. <u>However, Allan and colleagues' study relied on a mean sample size of only about 18 fish.</u> In a 2022 review of various scientists' conclusions about the impacts of ocean acidification on fish behavior, Josefin Sundin and colleagues caution that relying on such a relatively small sample size can increase the potential for biased analysis. Such analysis, in turn, can contribute to reports of exaggerated effects.
question_text: Which choice best describes the function of the underlined sentence in the text as a whole?

choices.label: A
choices.text: It introduces a detail about Allan and colleagues' study that is addressed in the sentences that follow.

choices.label: B
choices.text: It offers an alternative explanation for the behavior of *Pomacentrus amboinensis* that is described in the previous sentence.

choices.label: C
choices.text: It emphasizes the threat of ocean acidification to *Pomacentrus amboinensis* that is mentioned in the previous sentence.

choices.label: D
choices.text: It states the conclusion reached by Allan and colleagues that is elaborated on in the sentences that follow.

test_name: October 2025 US Form A SAT English Module 1
passage:
Which choice best describes the function of the underlined portion in the text as a whole?
Editor Jared Shurin's 2023 anthology *The Big Book of Cyberpunk* contains 108 stories, an enormous number when compared to the 1986 cyberpunk anthology *Mirrorshades*, which has only twelve. Encompassing both early (e.g., Fritz Leiber's 1950 story "Coming Attraction") and more recent (e.g., Erica Satifka's 2021 story "Act of Providence") contributions, <u>the broad chronological scope of Shurin's volume may make it seem more comprehensive than *Mirrorshades*,</u> but *Mirrorshades*'s focus is more carefully considered, rendering it the more authoritative anthology of this science fiction subgenre.
question_text: Which choice best describes the function of the underlined portion in the text as a whole?

choices.label: A
choices.text: It supports the assertion that Leiber and Satifka should be considered essential figures in the cyberpunk movement.

choices.label: B
choices.text: It indicates a sensible perception of a cyberpunk anthology that can nonetheless be disputed for a good reason.

choices.label: C
choices.text: It suggests that, because they are relatively up to date, more recently published collections of cyberpunk stories are likely to be more inclusive than earlier anthologies.

choices.label: D
choices.text: It helps explain why two compilers of anthologies have different ideas regarding which short stories should be labeled as "cyberpunk."

test_name: October 2025 US Form A SAT English Module 1
passage:
Which choice best states the function of the underlined portion in the text as a whole?
Wenheng Zhang and colleagues have explored how convergent evolution—<u>a phenomenon that occurs when the same trait evolves independently in two reproductively separate lineages</u>—can result from a genetic mechanism shared by both lineages. Meanwhile, Patricia J. Wittkopp and colleagues have investigated how convergence occurs through different genetic mechanisms, but the relative prevalence of convergence through shared and different genetic processes is still poorly understood. This motivated biologists Delbert A. Green II and Cassandra G. Extavour to evaluate both types of convergence in a single study for their 2012 paper.
question_text: Which choice best states the function of the underlined portion in the text as a whole?

choices.label: A
choices.text: It clarifies a concept that the author implies was unclear in the studies mentioned in the text.

choices.label: B
choices.text: It provides examples of how a phenomenon was studied by scientists in the field before Green and Extavour's study.

choices.label: C
choices.text: It introduces a method of scientific analysis that is discussed in greater detail later in the text.

choices.label: D
choices.text: It gives a basic description of a phenomenon that is central to the discussion that follows.

test_name: October 2025 US Form A SAT English Module 1
passage:
Which conclusion about steroid profiles is supported by information in both Text 1 and Text 2?
Text 1
As part of a study of ungulates (hoofed mammals) native to the Yellowstone River basin, fresh dung samples from identified individuals were collected in the Confederated Salish and Kootenai Tribes Bison Range of Montana. Based on steroidal analysis, on average the bison and elk samples had similar levels of beta-sitosterol and stigmastanol, which distinguished these species' profiles from that of moose.
Text 2
Although fecal-steroidal profile alone is not sufficient to distinguish between samples from bison and those from elk, the relative levels of zoostanols (steroids with animal origins), such as coprostanol and 24-ethyl-coprostanol, in sediments at Buffalo Ford Lake in Yellowstone National Park show that bison, elk, or both were the predominant ungulates in the lake's watershed for all strata of sediment examined, going as far back as 2,300 years.
question_text: Which conclusion about steroid profiles is supported by information in both Text 1 and Text 2?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The profiles for bison and elk contain similar concentrations of various steroids.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Each of the ungulate species in the Buffalo Ford Lake watershed has a distinctive and unique steroidal profile.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Some steroids in the bison and elk profiles are more likely than others to be preserved for long time spans.

choices.label: D
choices.text: The profiles of ungulates in and around the Yellowstone basin show that bison and elk have historically been more prevalent than moose in the area.

test_name: October 2025 US Form A SAT English Module 1
passage:
According to the text, where was the National Association of Black Journalists founded?
In 1942, Alice Dunnigan began working at the Associated Negro Press and *Chicago Defender*, eventually becoming the first Black woman correspondent to receive White House credentials. Dunnigan’s accomplishment is just one example of the rich history of Black journalism in the United States. That history is preserved by the National Association of Black Journalists, which was founded in Washington, DC, in 1975 to support Black media professionals and honor people like Dunnigan.
question_text: According to the text, where was the National Association of Black Journalists founded?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Philadelphia

choices.label: B
choices.text: Detroit

choices.label: C
choices.text: Washington, DC

choices.label: D
choices.text: San Diego

test_name: October 2025 US Form A SAT English Module 1
passage:
Based on the text, what is one likely effect of the change in baleen whale populations over the last century?
Baleen whales eat up to 30 percent of their total body mass in krill (tiny shrimplike creatures) per day. So in one day, a humpback whale weighing 30,000 kg could eat 9,000 kg, while a southern right whale weighing 80,000 kg could ingest a whopping 24,000 kg. Over the last century, baleen whale populations have declined, and contrary to some scientists' expectations, so have krill populations. Matthew S. Savoca and colleagues resolve this apparent discrepancy by pointing out that baleen whales cycle iron in the ocean, helping support phytoplankton populations, which, in turn, sustain krill populations.
question_text: Based on the text, what is one likely effect of the change in baleen whale populations over the last century?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The maximum body sizes of humpback and southern right whales have increased over the last century.

choices.label: B
choices.text: The quantity of krill needed to support a single southern right whale has increased over the last century.

choices.label: C
choices.text: The iron richness of the krill that baleen whales eat has declined over the last century.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Populations of phytoplankton that krill fed on have declined over the last century.

The first task in this investigation is to determine how the decline in Switzerland compares to that in other countries. The student finds that ______
question_text: Which choice most effectively uses data from the table to complete the statement?

choices.text: though the percent decrease in hours worked in Switzerland was less than that in Germany, it was greater than that in Brazil and Japan

choices.label: B

passage:
Which quotation from *The Age of Innocence* best illustrates the claim?
*The Age of Innocence* is a 1920 novel by Edith Wharton set in New York City in the 1870s. In the novel, Newland Archer attends an opera. Newland compares his intellect favorably to that of other men of New York City society who are in the audience: ______
question_text: Which quotation from *The Age of Innocence* best illustrates the claim?

choices.label: A
choices.text: "It surprised [Newland] that life should be going on in the old way when his own reactions to it had so completely changed."

choices.label: B
choices.text: "Newland Archer felt himself distinctly the superior of these chosen specimens of old New York gentry; he had probably read more, thought more, and even seen a good deal more of the world, than any other man of the number."

choices.label: C
choices.text: "No expense had been spared on the setting, which was acknowledged to be very beautiful even by people who shared [Newland's] acquaintance with the Opera houses of Paris and Vienna."

choices.label: D
choices.text: "Singly [the men around Newland] betrayed their inferiority; but grouped together they represented 'New York,' and the habit of masculine solidarity made him accept their doctrine on all the issues called moral."

test_name: October 2025 US Form A SAT English Module 1
passage:
Which finding, if true, would most directly weaken the student's hypothesis?
Paleontologist Amane Tajika and colleagues analyzed the shells of two marine mollusks called nautilids that were harvested from New Caledonia. The researchers found that samples from shell sections that formed before hatching, including sample F04, formed in 20-22°C water, whereas samples from sections that formed after hatching, including M27, formed in 13-15°C water. These findings are consistent with the temperatures Fijian nautilids encounter during their corresponding stages of development. Because water temperature varies with depth, a biology student hypothesized that the New Caledonian nautilids lay their eggs at approximately the same depth as Fijian nautilids do.
question_text: Which finding, if true, would most directly weaken the student's hypothesis?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Fijian nautilids generally live at lower depths than New Caledonian nautilids do, though Fijian nautilids migrate to the typical depth of New Caledonian nautilids to lay their eggs.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Once a nautilid hatches, it is a very mobile animal, so the temperature values reported by Tajika and colleagues for the adult nautilids are averages over a potentially large range of depths.

choices.label: C
choices.text: The water temperature at a given depth tends to be lower in waters near New Caledonia than it is at that depth in waters near Fiji.

choices.label: D
choices.text: There are few locations near Fiji with water temperatures of 13-15°C that are suitable for nautilid eggs.

test_name: October 2025 US Form A SAT English Module 1
passage:
Which choice most logically completes the text?
The *Mammillaria* cactus *M. guerreronis* occurs naturally only in the state of Guerrero in Mexico, and the smallness of its range makes it especially vulnerable to extinction. The traditional single-species approach to conservation emphasizes the need to focus on individual species most at risk, like *M. guerreronis*, but recently, conservationists have argued that an ecosystem-based approach that incorporates the many interactions between the climate, terrain, and various species of a given geographical area may lead to better outcomes for all the species in a given location. An ecosystem-based approach to the conservation of *M. guerreronis* could thus ______
question_text: Which choice most logically completes the text?

choices.label: A
choices.text: allow conservationists to better consider how climatic changes affecting Guerrero may reduce the number of species competing with *M. guerreronis*.

choices.label: B
choices.text: identify conservation strategies that may not be exclusively aimed at protecting *M. guerreronis* but that benefit the species nonetheless.

choices.label: C
choices.text: lead to a better understanding of how the distribution of *Mammillaria* species throughout Mexico has affected their survival.

choices.label: D
choices.text: be unable to evaluate whether *M. guerreronis* would be capable of surviving in habitats outside of Guerrero.

The student wants to emphasize the duration of López-Alegria and Williams's EVA. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:
• NASA mission Expedition 14 took place from September 18, 2006, to April 21, 2007.
• Astronauts Michael López-Alegria and Sunita Williams were crew members of Expedition 14.
• January 31, 2007: López-Alegria and Williams performed an extravehicular activity (EVA) to replace instruments on the Hubble Space Telescope.
• An EVA (also known as a spacewalk) is an activity done by astronauts outside the spacecraft.
• The duration of their EVA was 7 hours and 55 minutes.
• Theirs is counted among the longest completed spacewalks to date.
question_text: The student wants to emphasize the duration of López-Alegria and Williams's EVA. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Lopez-Alegria and Williams completed an EVA, also known as a spacewalk, in which they left the spacecraft to replace instruments on the Hubble Space Telescope.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Lasting 7 hours and 55 minutes, López-Alegría and Williams's EVA is one of the longest spacewalks completed to date.

choices.label: C
choices.text: During their time as crew members of Expedition 14, López-Alegria and Williams completed an EVA.

choices.label: D
choices.text: The EVA completed by Expedition 14 crew members López-Alegria and Williams took place from September 18, 2006, to April 21, 2007.

test_name: October 2025 US Form A SAT English Module 1
passage:
The student wants to contrast the locations of the two specimens. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:
• Cheryll is the nickname of a *Triceratops* fossil specimen housed at the Palm Beach Museum of Natural History.
• The Palm Beach Museum of Natural History is located in Wellington, Florida.
• Cheryll lived in the Late Cretaceous period.
• Dio is the nickname of a *Triceratops* fossil specimen housed at the Royal Ontario Museum.
• The Royal Ontario Museum is located in Ontario, Canada.
• Dio lived in the Late Cretaceous period.
question_text: The student wants to contrast the locations of the two specimens. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Cheryll is housed at the Palm Beach Museum of Natural History in Wellington, Florida, while Dio is housed at the Royal Ontario Museum in Ontario, Canada.

choices.label: B
choices.text: The *Triceratops* fossil specimen Cheryll is not the only such specimen currently housed in a museum or institute.

choices.label: C
choices.text: The Royal Ontario Museum in Ontario, Canada, houses Dio, a *Triceratops* fossil specimen.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Like Cheryll, Dio is a *Triceratops* fossil specimen that lived in the Late Cretaceous period.
